text.title: DDD Domain Events Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to introduce a new domain event end-to-end.

step.name: name-the-event
step.action: Name the event as past-tense fact (`OrderPlaced`, `PaymentCaptured`, `SubscriptionRenewed`). Avoid procedural names (`PlaceOrderEvent`, `OnOrderPlaced`).

step.name: define-record
step.action: Define frozen record with `event_id`, `occurred_at`, aggregate identity, and only the payload downstream consumers need. Avoid leaking the full aggregate state.

step.name: raise-in-command
step.action: Append the event to `self._events` inside the command method, after invariants pass. Add a unit test asserting the event is raised exactly once per successful command.

step.name: collect-after-commit
step.action: In the application service: open transaction → call aggregate command → commit → `events = aggregate.collect_events()` → dispatch. If exception before commit, events are discarded automatically.
step.decision-gate: If events are dispatched before commit, FAIL the review.

step.name: outbox-or-in-process
step.action: In-process: pass events to in-memory dispatcher. Broker: insert into `outbox` table inside the transaction; relay process picks up unprocessed rows and publishes with dedup by `event_id`. Add a contract test against the outbox shape.
